title: Easy Rolling Storage Cart
summary: A three-shelf cart on casters from plywood and framing lumber. Roll it wherever the work is — garage, craft room, kitchen.
description:
A rolling cart is one of those builds that pays for itself the first week — three open shelves you can wheel to wherever you're working and park out of the way after. This one is honest and cheap: two ladder-like leg frames tied together by shelf boxes, with a plywood panel dropped into each, all riding on four casters.

The one spec that actually matters is the plywood thickness, and it's a real load decision: 1/4" for light storage, 1/2" if the shelves will carry tools, paint cans, or anything heavy. Undersize it and a loaded middle shelf bows. The shelf frames underneath the plywood are what carry the weight, so build those square and let the plywood just be the deck.

Ripping the sheet into three 15-3/4" x 48" panels is the first move — a table saw makes it painless, or have the panels cut at the store. After that it's screws, glue, and finish nails. Add casters last and check that all four sit flat so it doesn't rock.

tools.note: Or a table saw to rip the plywood into shelf panels.
Or hammer and finish nails for the plywood decks.

materials.name: Hardwood plywood, 4 ft x 4 ft
materials.note: 1/4" for light loads, 1/2" for heavy. Shelf decks.

materials.name: Caster wheels, 2" diameter
materials.note: With mounting screws. Two locking casters are a nice upgrade.

steps.title: Rip the plywood into shelf panels
steps.body: Rip the plywood into three panels, each 15-3/4" wide x 48" long. Set a table-saw fence to 15-3/4" and run the sheet through, or measure, mark, and cut with a circular saw — or have the store make the cuts.

steps.title: Build the two leg frames
steps.body: Assemble the two ladder-like leg frames from the 2x4 legs and shelf supports with 2-1/2" self-tapping screws (or pocket holes if you have a jig). Build them identical so the shelves land level.

steps.title: Build the shelf boxes
steps.body: Build the frame for each shelf first from the 1x3 ends and sides — nail and glue, use 2" screws, or pocket holes. Then apply glue to the bottom edges and nail the plywood deck onto each frame.

steps.title: Assemble the cart
steps.body: Set the shelves between the two leg frames at your chosen heights and screw them down, also screwing into the legs. Check the whole cart for square as you go.

steps.title: Add wheels and finish
steps.body: Attach the four casters to the base, making sure all four sit flat so the cart doesn't rock. Finish with stain or paint if you like — bare is fine for a shop cart.

images.alt: A three-shelf rolling storage cart on casters made from plywood and framing lumber
